title: sphenostylis erecta subsp. erecta
intro: Sphenostylis erecta subsp. erecta, also known as the "Wild Potato", is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Leguminosae. It has been traditionally used in African medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Sphenostylis erecta subsp. erecta include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic and a digestive aid. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion or decoction, with the root bark being used to make a herbal tea or tincture.
